# Bundled third-party Python packages

These are pure-Python source trees vendored into the app bundle so that
plugins can `import requests` / `import cachetools` without an on-device
package manager.

Currently shipped:

| Package | Version | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `requests`            | 2.34.x | depends on the items below |
| `urllib3`             | 2.7.x  | required by `requests` |
| `certifi`             | 2026.x | trust store (`cacert.pem`) |
| `charset_normalizer`  | 3.4.x  | required by `requests`; mypyc native speedups stripped — pure-Python `md.py` fallback is used |
| `idna`                | 3.16.x | required by `requests` |
| `cachetools`          | 7.1.x  | LRU/TTL cache primitives |

## Updating

Run from the repo root, then strip native artifacts:

```sh
SITE=exteraGram/EGPluginEngine/Python/site-packages
pip3 install --target="$SITE" requests cachetools
find "$SITE" \( -name "*.so" -o -name "*.pyd" -o -name "*.dylib" \) -delete
find "$SITE" -name "__pycache__" -type d -exec rm -rf {} +
rm -rf "$SITE/bin"
```

`*.so` files are platform-specific (built for the host that pip ran on)
and have no value on iOS — pure-Python fallbacks are always shipped
alongside them in the upstream packages.

## sys.path order

The bundled site-packages directory is appended to `sys.path` *after*
the user-writable site-packages at `Documents/EGPlugins/site-packages`.
Plugins that drop a newer version of e.g. `requests` into the writable
location will override the bundled copy.
